prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear, a CPU is like the little brain inside a computer. It’s the part that does all the thinking and tells everything else what to do, kind of like when I give you instructions to bake cookies. It’s very fast, much faster than I can even count! Without the CPU, the computer wouldn’t know how to work or follow your games and programs. So, even though I don’t understand all the tiny bits inside, I know it’s a very important piece that helps the whole machine run smoothly.
